workflow.name: Create SEO-optimised content from Google Sheets keywords with DataForSEO and Ollama
workflow.description:
This workflow creates content for your primary keywords in a Google Sheet by using SERP data and natural language skills of LLMs to create content at scale.

Steps:
1) Fetch Rows from a Google Sheet with keywords in Column 'Keyword'
2) Use DataforSEO API (or any other SERP API) to fetch top results, AI Overview, related searches, PAA, etc., and compile them for a prompt.
3) Feed the SERP Data and SEO Guidelines to an AI Agent with a locally run LLM Model (to save cost) to create the content.
4) Add the data in the same Google Sheet in the column header 'Output'.

parameters.content:
## Keyword-based Content Writer
## Keyword-based Content Writer
This workflow creates content for your primary keywords in a Google Sheet by using SERP data and natural language skills of LLMs to create content at scale.


Say, you have a programmatic SEO Strategy in mind with about 1000 unique keywords. You need to create content for each of them and take live, but you are cautious about not breaking the helpful content policy. 

How do you create helpful content at scale quickly?

1) This workflow fetches the top 10 results, AI Overviews, related searches, and People Also Ask questions for each of your keywords and compiles them in a nice prompt.

2) LLMs have strict rules in place to ensure tone, readability, language, SEO best practices and work limit, which can serve as guidelines for you to customise with more levers.

3) It updates all the content in the sheet. All you need to do is maintain the headers.

4) It can create content for thousands of pages without spending any money by using locally run LLMs and free 5 USD credits from DataforSEO in corporate accounts. 


**Use Cases**

1) pSEO Pages creation

2) Blog writing based on SERP Data at scale for short-form content

3) Write SEO Optimised content for product and brand pages based on SERP Sentiment.

Pre-Requisites:

Google Sheets OAuth
DataForSEO account
Ollama installed locally
MongoDB connection

## Step 1: Adding your Google Sheet 
**Create a Google Sheet with the following column headers 

'Keyword' - Column with keywords

'Output' - Column where you want your content to be added**

Nodes:

The 'Get row(s) in sheet' Node requires you to connect your Google Account Credentials (Oauth credentials or service accounts) with Google Sheet API & Google Drive API

The Filter Node keeps the rows where the output is empty.

Loop Over Items Node to loop over each row in the sheet

## Step 2: Fetching SERP results
**Using the DataforSEO API, this node captures the following SERP results data:

1. Top Search Results
2. AI Overviews
3. People Also Ask (PAA)
4. Related Searches**


Nodes:

HTTP Request Node:
1) This node calls the DataforSEO API to fetch the data. Create an account using your work email to get 5 USD in Credits, which are more than enough to start making hundreds of requests.
Check out: https://dataforseo.com/blog/a-kickstart-guide-to-using-dataforseo-apis

2) Code node cleans the output and compiles it into text for the prompt.

3) If Node checks if the HTTP Request Status Code is 200, and throws an error in the Google Sheet if not.

## Step 3: Setting up AI Agent 
**AI Agent has 2 major parts, AI Model to run and Storage**

AI Model: I recommend using a local LLM model like Ollama 3.2 to save money on API tokens, which is good at creating content and can run well with an M1 Chip & 16GB RAM. Check out: https://docs.ollama.com/quickstart

Database: I have used MongoDB as storage. It is easy to configure and has a generous free tier. Use the Connection String Method to add your account.

Check out: https://docs.n8n.io/integrations/builtin/app-nodes/n8n-nodes-base.mongodb/


Nodes:

AI Agent Node to add a prompt and create content.

Code Node to clean the output in HTML format. You can change the final format as you want. This one is HTML.

Update Row in Sheet Node to add the final content in the respective row. Make sure to check that the matching column matches the Keyword, and the Update column should be the same as the Output Column Header.

value.HTML: HTTP Node Failed to fetch results

nodes.name: Fetch Rows from your Google Sheet
Filter rows that are already populated
Fetch SERP Results + AI Mode Answers
Compile SERP Data into one Output for Prompt

parameters.text:
=Using the {{ $('Loop Over Items').item.json.Keyword }},{{ $json.compiled_text }} , write 200 words on the topic and compile your findings in a blog format with no more than 500 words. Follow the following blog guidelines:

1) keep the language simple but casual. 

2) write in humansied format.

3) add a table with locations.

4) optimise for the keyword for good SEO.

5) no keyword stuffing.

6) give the output in html formatting.

7) stick to the topic and search like how a user would search on google.

8) title should  be in the format "Where was {movie name} shot?"

9) add FAQs to optimise for SEO.

10) follow a typical blog format with introduction, body and conclusion

11) Return ONLY raw HTML. Do NOT use any JSON wrapper, function call format, 
tool call format, or code fences. Start your response directly with <!DOCTYPE html>

main.node: Compile SERP Data into one Output for Prompt
Fetch SERP Results + AI Mode Answers

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.

data.details: The Code node allows you to execute JavaScript in your workflow.

The Filter node can be used to filter items based on a condition. If the condition is met, the item will be passed on to the next node. If the condition is not met, the item will be omitted. Conditions can be combined together by AND(meet all conditions), or OR(meet at least one condition).
